args.code:
import json

# Business data
businesses = [
    {"name": "City Textile", "gmap_id": "gmap_44", "num_of_reviews": "6"}, 
    {"name": "San Soo Dang", "gmap_id": "gmap_41", "num_of_reviews": "18"}, 
    {"name": "Nova Fabrics", "gmap_id": "gmap_43", "num_of_reviews": "6"}, 
    {"name": "Nobel Textile Co", "gmap_id": "gmap_38", "num_of_reviews": "7"}, 
    {"name": "Beads and More", "gmap_id": "gmap_39", "num_of_reviews": "18"}, 
    {"name": "Ace Jewelry & Loan", "gmap_id": "gmap_42", "num_of_reviews": "7"}, 
    {"name": "Widows Peak Salon", "gmap_id": "gmap_40", "num_of_reviews": "35"}
]

# Rating data
ratings = [
    {"gmap_id": "gmap_38", "avg_rating": 4.285714285714286}, 
    {"gmap_id": "gmap_39", "avg_rating": 3.2222222222222223}, 
    {"gmap_id": "gmap_40", "avg_rating": 4.857142857142857}, 
    {"gmap_id": "gmap_41", "avg_rating": 4.277777777777778}, 
    {"gmap_id": "gmap_42", "avg_rating": 3.2857142857142856}, 
    {"gmap_id": "gmap_43", "avg_rating": 3.3333333333333335}, 
    {"gmap_id": "gmap_44", "avg_rating": 4.5}
]

# Convert ratings to dict for easy lookup
rating_dict = {r["gmap_id"]: r["avg_rating"] for r in ratings}

# Merge
merged_data = []
for b in businesses:
    g_id = b["gmap_id"]
    if g_id in rating_dict:
        merged_data.append({
            "name": b["name"],
            "avg_rating": rating_dict[g_id]
        })

# Sort by avg_rating descending
sorted_data = sorted(merged_data, key=lambda x: x["avg_rating"], reverse=True)

# Get top 5
top_5 = sorted_data[:5]

print("__RESULT__:")
print(json.dumps(top_5))

import json

# Business data
businesses = [
    {"name": "City Textile", "gmap_id": "gmap_44", "num_of_reviews": "6"}, 
    {"name": "San Soo Dang", "gmap_id": "gmap_41", "num_of_reviews": "18"}, 
    {"name": "Nova Fabrics", "gmap_id": "gmap_43", "num_of_reviews": "6"}, 
    {"name": "Nobel Textile Co", "gmap_id": "gmap_38", "num_of_reviews": "7"}, 
    {"name": "Beads and More", "gmap_id": "gmap_39", "num_of_reviews": "18"}, 
    {"name": "Ace Jewelry & Loan", "gmap_id": "gmap_42", "num_of_reviews": "7"}, 
    {"name": "Widows Peak Salon", "gmap_id": "gmap_40", "num_of_reviews": "35"}
]

# Rating data
ratings = [
    {"gmap_id": "gmap_38", "avg_rating": 4.285714285714286}, 
    {"gmap_id": "gmap_39", "avg_rating": 3.2222222222222223}, 
    {"gmap_id": "gmap_40", "avg_rating": 4.857142857142857}, 
    {"gmap_id": "gmap_41", "avg_rating": 4.277777777777778}, 
    {"gmap_id": "gmap_42", "avg_rating": 3.2857142857142856}, 
    {"gmap_id": "gmap_43", "avg_rating": 3.3333333333333335}, 
    {"gmap_id": "gmap_44", "avg_rating": 4.5}
]

# Convert ratings to dict for easy lookup
rating_dict = {r["gmap_id"]: r["avg_rating"] for r in ratings}

# Merge
merged_data = []
for b in businesses:
    g_id = b["gmap_id"]
    if g_id in rating_dict:
        merged_data.append({
            "name": b["name"],
            "avg_rating": rating_dict[g_id]
        })

# Sort by avg_rating descending
sorted_data = sorted(merged_data, key=lambda x: x["avg_rating"], reverse=True)

# Get top 5
top_5 = sorted_data[:5]

print("__RESULT__:")
print(json.dumps(top_5))
